schemes.name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme
schemes.ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
schemes.tagline: Get up to ₹20 lakh in grants and ₹50 lakh in investment to validate your idea, build a prototype, and enter the market.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme helps new startups get early funding so they can turn ideas into real products and grow, filling the gap when they typically struggle to find initial investment.
schemes.objectives: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) aims to provide financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry and commercialisation. Through this funding support, startups graduate to a level where they will be able to raise investments from angel investors or venture capitalists or seek loans from commercial banks or financial institutions
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups.
Incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application.

Startup must have a business idea to develop a product or a service with market fit, viable commercialisation, and scope of scaling. Startup should be using technology in its core product or service, or business model.
Startup should not have received more than ₹10 lakh of monetary support under any other Central or State Government scheme
benefits: Up to ₹20 lakh as grant for validation of Proof of Concept, or prototype development, or product trials.
Up to ₹50 lakh of investment for market entry, commercialisation, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt or debt-linked instruments
schemes.howToApply: Visit https://seedfund.startupindia.gov.in/ to apply. Last date for startups to apply under SISFS was 31 May 2026. Disbursals are ongoing.
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹20 lakh (grant); up to ₹50 lakh (convertible debt)

schemes.name: Fund of Funds for Startups
schemes.ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
schemes.tagline: A ₹10,000 crore fund that backs SEBI-registered venture funds (AIFs), which in turn invest equity in DPIIT-recognised startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) provides capital to venture funds so they can invest in startups, helping increase the availability of funding and support the growth of India’s startup ecosystem.
schemes.objectives: The Government has established FFS to catalyze venture capital investments. It is operationalized by Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI), which provides capital to SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) who in turn invest in startups through equity and equity-linked instruments.
eligibility: SEBI-registered AIFs can apply for capital under the Scheme.
The funding is provided to DPIIT recognised startups by selected AIFs.
benefits: The Scheme has a corpus of ₹10,000 crore, which is aimed to be provided to startup through AIFs. The AIFs supported under the Scheme are to invest at least twice the contribution out of FFS in startups across stages
schemes.howToApply:
The application process for AIFs has been digitalised and a detailed application can be submitted by the AIFs on SIDBI’s Fund Application Portal (https://vcfapplication.sidbi.in/login). The AIF applications undergo an eligibility screening and screening based on preliminary questionnaire.
Eligible startups can approach selected AIFs for investments.
schemes.howMuch: Corpus ₹10,000 crore; startup-level investment varies by AIF

schemes.name: Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0
schemes.ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
schemes.tagline: A new ₹10,000 crore fund of funds channeling capital through AIFs into deep tech, manufacturing and other priority startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 supports startups by channeling government capital into venture funds to boost investment in emerging businesses, aiming to fuel innovation in priority sectors.
schemes.objectives:
The Government operationalized the Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 (Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 (FoF 2.0)) with a total corpus of ₹10,000 crore. Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI), Implementation Agency (IA) of the FFS 1.0, shall also be the Implementing Agency for the Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 (FoF 2.0).
In addition, another domestic IA(s) shall also be selected to implement the proposed Scheme. The IA(s) will provide capital to SEBI-registered Category I and Category II Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) who in turn will invest in startups through equity and equity-linked instruments.
Investments under Startup India FoF 2.0 AIFs supporting priority segments including deep tech startups, early growth stage startups supported by smaller AIFs, technology-driven and innovative manufacturing startups, and sector or stage agnostic startups.
eligibility: SEBI-registered AIFs can apply for capital under the Scheme.
The funding is provided to DPIIT recognised startups by selected AIFs.
benefits: The Scheme has a corpus of ₹10,000 crore, which is aimed to be provided to startup through AIFs. AIFs supported under the Scheme are required to invest in startups across stages, adhering to the minimum investment multiplier for various segments as defined in the Operational Guidelines.
schemes.howToApply:
The application process for AIFs has been digitalised and a detailed application can be submitted by the AIFs on SIDBI’s Fund Application Portal (https://vcfapplication.sidbi.in/login). The AIF applications undergo an eligibility screening and screening based on preliminary questionnaire.
Eligible startups can approach selected AIFs for investments.
schemes.howMuch: Corpus ₹10,000 crore; startup-level investment varies

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups
schemes.ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
schemes.tagline: Government guarantees up to ₹20 crore on loans to DPIIT-recognised startups, so lenders can fund you without collateral.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) helps startups access loans by assuring lenders that the government will cover part of the risk, making it easier for young businesses to get funding without collateral.
schemes.objectives: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) aims at providing credit guarantee up to a specified limit against loans extended by Member Institutions (MIs) to finance eligible borrowers viz. entities recognised as ‘startups’ by DPIIT. CGSS is operated by the National Credit Guarantee Trustee Company (NCGTC) Limited.
eligibility: Eligible (i) Scheduled Commercial Banks and Financial Institutions; (ii) RBI registered Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs) and (iii) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) can become member institutions (MIs) subject to requirements.
Eligible borrowers are DPIIT recognised startups.
benefits: Maximum guarantee of ₹20 crore or actual outstanding credit amount, whichever is less. For both transaction-based and umbrella-based cover.
schemes.howToApply:
Visit the website for details: https://www.ncgtc.in/en/product-details/CGSS/Credit-Guarantee%20Scheme%20for%20Start-ups%20(CGSS)
For application through the Jan Samarth portal: https://www.jansamarth.in/business-loan-startup-scheme.
schemes.howMuch: Guarantee cover up to ₹20 crore per eligible borrower

schemes.name: Scheme for Facilitating Startups Intellectual Property Protection
schemes.ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
schemes.tagline: Free IP facilitator support to file patents, trademarks and designs — startups pay only statutory government fees.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Scheme for Facilitating Startups Intellectual Property Protection (SIPP) helps startups protect their ideas by providing support and cost relief for filing patents, trademarks, and designs, encouraging innovation and safeguarding ownership.
schemes.objectives:
To protect and promote Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) of Startups and to encourage innovation and creativity among them, a Scheme for facilitating Start-Ups Intellectual Property Protection (SIPP) has been launched.
It facilitates startups in filing and processing of their patent, design or trademark application through the assistance of IP Facilitators, whose fee was borne by the Office of the Controller General of Patents Designs and Trademarks, Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade, Government of India.
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups.
Applicants must self-declare that they have not availed funds under any other government scheme for paying facilitator/patent agent/trademark agent fees for the same IP application.
benefits: Support for filing and processing patent, trademark and design applications.
Professional fees of facilitators are paid/reimbursed by the office of CGPDTM; startups generally bear only statutory government fees.
End-to-end assistance including advisory, drafting, filing, responses to examination reports, hearings, opposition handling and final disposal of IP applications.
schemes.howToApply: Visit IP India SIPP Page / Facilitator Details: https://ipindia.gov.in/page-content/startups-intellectual-property-protection-sipp

links.label: IP India SIPP Page / Facilitator Details

schemes.howMuch: 80% rebate on patent statutory fees; 50% rebate on trademark/design fees

schemes.name: GeM Startup Runway
schemes.tagline: Sell your innovative products directly to government buyers on GeM, with exemptions from prior turnover, experience and EMD requirements.
schemes.whatIsThis: The GeM Startup Runway enables startups to showcase and sell their innovative products directly to government buyers, helping them access public procurement opportunities and grow their business.
schemes.objectives: GeM Startup Runway helps startups sell innovative products and services directly to government buyers through the Government e-Marketplace (GeM) by promoting solutions that are unique in concept, design, process, or functionality and improve startup participation in public procurement.
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups registered as sellers/service providers on GeM.

Startups with products/services in existing GeM categories or in dedicated startup sectors such as AI, robotics, agritech, cleantech, cybersecurity, edtech, health tech, water tech, assistive tech, etc.
Other innovative entities.
benefits: Access to a large base of government buyers across ministries, departments, PSUs and other public entities.
Exemption/relaxation from requirements such as prior turnover, prior experience, and Earnest Money Deposit (EMD).
Dedicated marketplace visibility for startups and innovative products.
Ability to list innovative products even when standard categories may not exist.
schemes.howToApply: Visit GeM Startup Runway: https://gem.gov.in/Startup_Runway
schemes.howMuch: Platform for showcasing innovations for public procurement

schemes.name: NIDHI – Seed Support Program (SSP)
schemes.tagline: Seed support through NIDHI STEPs/TBIs for incubated DPIIT startups to fund product development, testing, trials and mentoring.
schemes.whatIsThis: The NIDHI – Seed Support Program (SSP) provides early-stage funding and support to innovators to help them develop and validate their ideas, encouraging the creation of viable startups.
schemes.objectives: NIDHI-Seed Support Program (SSP) aims to provide seed support to deserving incubatee startups, through NIDHI Science & Technology Entrepreneurs Parks (STEPs) and NIDHI Technology Business Incubators (TBIs).
eligibility: Incubatee should be a DPIIT recognised startup with minimum of three months of residency at the STEPs/TBIs. The shareholding by Indian promoters in the incubatee startup should be at least 51%.
benefits: Under the program, the startups are supported primarily for product development, testing and trials, test marketing, mentoring, professional consultancy (to attract professors/experts from institutions to work with startups.), IPR issues and any other area as deemed necessary and recommended by the Seed Support Management Committee of STEP/TBIs.
schemes.howToApply: Applications details are released on the website https://nidhi.dst.gov.in/nidhissp/
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹1 crore per startup, subject to TBI guidelines

schemes.name: NIDHI – Promotion and Acceleration of Young and Aspiring Technology Entrepreneurs (PRAYAS) 2.0
schemes.tagline: Prototyping grants of up to ₹20 lakh (₹40 lakh at deep-tech APCs) for innovators and young startups via PRAYAS Centres.
schemes.whatIsThis: The NIDHI PRAYAS 2.0 scheme provides early-stage funding and prototyping support to young innovators, helping them turn their technology ideas into working products and viable startups.
schemes.objectives: The scheme aims to bridge the gap funding in the very early-stage idea/ proof of concept. Building on the outcomes of NIDHI PRAYAS 1.0, the programme has evolved into NIDHI PRAYAS 2.0, with enhanced support for both technology-based and deep-tech innovations. It is a pre-incubation initiative that is implemented through incubators known as the PRAYAS Centres (PCs) and Advance PRAYAS Centres (APCs) (specialized centres to support DeepTech innovations).
eligibility: A DPIIT recognised startup or an individual innovator are eligible to apply under the program.
In case of a recognised startup, it has to be incorporated in India, not more than 5 years of existence and its annual turnover for any financial year should not have exceeded ₹1 crore.
benefits: Level 1 – PCs: Each innovator/startup supported by a Level 1 Centre PC may receive up to ₹20 Lakhs grant for prototyping. DST provides up to ₹2 crore per annum to each PC.
Level 2 – APCs: Provides prototyping grants of up to ₹40 lakh per innovator/ startup. DST provides up to ₹4 crore per annum to each APC.
schemes.howToApply: Applicant incubators can submit the proposal online in the prescribed proforma, at the NIDHI PRAYAS PMU Portal https://nidhi-prayas.org/ or on https://nidhi.dst.gov.in/schemes- programmes/nidhiprayas/
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹40 lakh through PRAYAS 2.0 calls

schemes.name: Research, Development & Innovation (RDI) Scheme
schemes.tagline: Debt and equity funding covering 50% of R&D project costs or funding rounds, via fund managers under a ₹1 lakh crore scheme.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Research, Development & Innovation (RDI) Scheme provides funding and support to help organizations create and improve new technologies or ideas so they can boost innovation, economic growth, and global competitiveness.
schemes.objectives:
DST has launched the Research, Development, and Innovation (RDI) Scheme, approved by the Cabinet on 1st July 2025, with a total outlay of ₹1 lakh crore.
The initiative aims to catalyze private sector participation in high-impact R&D and strengthen India’s capabilities in strategic technologies and promote technological self-reliance, aligning with the nation’s long-term innovation and Atmanirbhar Bharat vision.
eligibility: The RDI Fund is provided to Second Level Fund Managers (SLFM) which include Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), Development Finance Institutions (DFIs), Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs), and Focused Research Organisations (FROs).
The SLFM then provides funds to private companies including DPIIT recognised startups advancing RDI-intensive technologies.
benefits: Loan – Debt funding (via loan or optionally convertible debt) from the SLFM, calculated as 50% of the project cost.
Equity – Funding via equity or equity linked instruments from the SLFM, calculated as 50% of the value of each funding round.
schemes.howToApply:
Visit - https://rdifund.anrf.gov.in/images/pdf/RDIF-%20AIF_and_FOF.pdf
Startups can apply directly through the SLFMs, for instance the Technology Development Board (TDB) (https://www.e-techcom.tdb.gov.in/rdif-registration.php

links.label: RDIF AIF and FOF (How to Apply)

schemes.maxFunding: Up to 50% of project cost / funding round
schemes.howMuch: Scheme corpus ₹1 lakh crore; project-level support varies

schemes.name: National Quantum Mission (NQM)
schemes.tagline: Seed, prototype and scale-up funding for quantum technology startups and R&D routed through NQM Thematic Hubs.
schemes.whatIsThis: The National Quantum Mission (NQM) supports research and development of advanced quantum technologies (like quantum computing and secure communication) so India can build cutting-edge capabilities and stay competitive in future tech.
schemes.objectives:
The National Quantum Mission (NQM) aims to seed, nurture and scale up scientific and industrial R&D and create a vibrant & innovative ecosystem in Quantum Technology (QT) in the country.
As part of the mission, four Thematic Hubs (T-Hubs) will be established as Section-8 Companies, not-for-profit entities, within premier academic institutions and national research and development labs. These T-Hubs will specialize in four distinct technology verticals: Quantum Computing, Quantum Communication, Quantum Sensing & Metrology, and Quantum Materials & Devices.
eligibility: Academic institutions, R&D labs, and consortia working in quantum computing, communication, sensing/metrology, and materials/devices.
Startups working in quantum technology, preferably DPIIT-registered and Indian-owned/controlled as per startup support guidelines.
For teaching lab support, eligible institutions include Government-funded/aided technical institutions/universities and eligible AICTE/UGC-recognised institutions meeting quality benchmarks.
benefits: Funding and institutional support for quantum R&D, prototypes, product development, and commercialisation through NQM T-Hubs.
Startup support can include seed/prototype funding and scale-up support routed through T-Hubs/TIHs.
Creation of skilled manpower, research infrastructure, and industry-academia collaboration in quantum technologies.
schemes.howToApply:
Startups apply through online calls/challenges; applications are evaluated on need, feasibility, impact, novelty, team strength, and fund-utilization plan.
Other applicants apply through DST/NQM calls or the relevant Thematic Hub (T-Hub) based on the focus area
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹25 crore per startup; mission outlay ₹6,003.65 crore (FY2024–FY2031)

schemes.name: Promoting Innovations in Individuals, Start-ups and MSMEs
schemes.tagline: Grants for individual innovators, startups and MSMEs to build prototypes and commercialise ideas — up to ₹50 lakh in project support.
schemes.whatIsThis: The PRISM scheme provides funding and support to individual innovators, startups, and small businesses so they can turn new ideas into useful products and grow their businesses.
schemes.objectives:
PRISM (Promoting Innovations in Individuals, Start-ups and MSMEs) Scheme supports individual innovators with financial assistance that helps them build prototypes, develop technologies, and move toward patenting & commercialisation.
DSIR has established a robust mechanism to implement this scheme across the country through TePP Outreach cum Cluster Innovation Centres (TOCICs) in Universities and National research laboratories and PRISM Advisory and Screening Committee PASC at Delhi to receive and evaluate the proposals from innovators for funding.
eligibility: Public funded institutions or organizations viz. Autonomous Organizations or Society registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860 or Indian Trusts Act, 1882 engaged in promotion of innovation. Any Indian citizen having an innovative idea and wish to translate their idea into working prototypes/models/processes.
benefits: Support of ₹12 lakh per annum would be given to TOCICs to cover expenses. In addition, 10% of the grants released to the innovators/autonomous organisation/R&D labs etc. supported by the agency/TOCICs will be given to the agency per project for monitoring the project execution. For innovators:
Phase I: Category-I Up to ₹2 lakh or 90% of the approved project cost
Phase I: Category II - Up to ₹20 lakh or 90% of the approved project cost
Phase II: Up to ₹50 lakh or 50% of the approved project cost
PRISM-R&D Proposals: Maximum support may be up to ₹50 lakh, limited to 50% of the total project cost for developing technology solutions aimed at helping MSME clusters.
schemes.howToApply: Website: https://www.dsir.gov.in/offerings/schemes-and-services/details/promoting-innovations-in-individuals-start-ups-and-msmes-prism-kjMwITMtQWa
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹50 lakh depending on PRISM phase/category

schemes.name: Biotechnology Ignition Grant Scheme
schemes.ministry: Department of Biotechnology / Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC)
schemes.tagline: Grant-in-aid of up to ₹50 lakh over 18 months for biotech startups and entrepreneurs to turn scientific ideas into products.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Biotechnology Ignition Grant (BIG) Scheme provides early-stage funding and support to biotech innovators and startups so they can turn promising scientific ideas into viable products and businesses.
schemes.objectives: Biotechnology Ignition Grant (BIG) is a flagship programme of BIRAC, which provides support to young startups and entrepreneurial individuals. BIG is the largest early-stage biotech funding programme in India.
eligibility: Individual Entrepreneurs and startups
The applicant must be incubated in an incubator (may/may not be supported by BIRAC).
benefits: Grant-in-aid of up to ₹50 lakh provided to startup for a period of up to 18 months.

During the BIG tenure, BIRAC extends several networking opportunities to connect with experts, mentors, industry, investors, peer to peer learning, specialized trainings & workshops, exposure to various platforms on merit basis
schemes.howToApply: BIRAC issues a national call inviting applications under BIG at least twice a year (typically on 1st of January & 1st of July). The Call for Proposals is typically open for a period of 30-45 days. Application for BIG needs to be submitted online only through BIRAC website www.birac.nic.in
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹50 lakh for up to 18 months

schemes.name: Biotechnology Innovation Fund – Accelerating Entrepreneurs
schemes.ministry: Department of Biotechnology / Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC)
schemes.tagline: BIRAC fund of funds whose partner AIFs invest up to ₹7 crore equity (plus ₹3.5 crore follow-on) in scaling biotech startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Biotechnology Innovation Fund – Accelerating Entrepreneurs (AcE) provides financial support to biotech startups so they can develop and scale innovative solutions faster and bring them to market.
schemes.objectives: The AcE Fund is a ‘fund of funds’ that aims to partner with fund managers (AcE Fund Partners) and invest in SEBI-registered AIFs (Daughter Funds). AcE Daughter Funds commit investment of 2x BIRAC’s contribution into biotech startups.
eligibility: AcE Fund invests and partners with SEBI-registered Category I and II AIFs in biotech sector.
Startups and SMEs in subsectors such as Healthcare, Life Sciences, Pharma, Biopharma, Med-tech, Diagnostics, Industrial-Biotech, Agri-tech, Bio-IT/Services and allied areas.
benefits: The AcE Fund shall make a maximum capital commitment of up to ₹30 crore or up to 30% of the total aggregate capital commitment amount (i.e. fund corpus) in each Daughter Fund.
Ticket size of up to ₹7 crore, with provision of additional follow-on funding up to ₹3.5 crore is provided to an eligible startup.
schemes.howToApply: Apply on: https://birac.nic.in/aceFund.php
schemes.maxFunding: Up to ₹7 crore per startup (plus follow-on up to ₹3.5 crore)
schemes.howMuch: Startup-level investment varies; fund operates through selected AIFs

schemes.name: BIRAC Incubator SEED Fund
schemes.ministry: Department of Biotechnology / Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC)
schemes.tagline: Seed equity funding of up to ₹30 lakh, routed through incubators, for biotech, med-tech and life sciences startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: The BIRAC Incubator SEED Fund (SEED) provides early-stage funding through incubators to biotech startups so they can develop their ideas into viable products and launch successful businesses.
schemes.objectives: BIRAC Sustainable Entrepreneurship and Enterprise Development Fund (“SEED Fund”) primarily addresses the financial support requirements of technology startups in their initial phase of operations. Funding is disbursed to startups under this program through the incubators. The funding support aims to enhance startups’ ability to raise investments from Angel/Venture capitalist or to seek loans from commercial banks /financial institutions.
eligibility: Biotech/Med-Tech/Life Sciences startups.
benefits: Seed funding as equity and equity linked instruments up to ₹30 lakh per startup.
schemes.howToApply: Refer to the BIRAC Incubator SEED Fund Scheme website for more information of the incubators associated with the program: https://www.birac.nic.in/seedFundNew.php
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹30 lakh per startup; incubator corpus typically ~₹2 crore

schemes.name: LEAP Fund – Launching Entrepreneurial Driven Affordable Products
schemes.tagline: Equity funding above ₹30 lakh and up to ₹1 crore per startup to help biotech startups pilot and commercialise products.
schemes.whatIsThis: The LEAP Fund supports entrepreneurs in creating affordable, innovative products so more people can access useful technologies while encouraging business growth.
schemes.objectives: LEAP Fund assists potential biotech startups in piloting & commercialising their products & technologies enabling them to raise venture capital investment.
eligibility: Startups in Biotech/med-tech/life science sectors.
benefits: Funding support of more than ₹30 lakh and up to ₹1 crore per startup. Startup eligible and suitable for up to the threshold of ₹30 lakh investment support will be considered under SEED fund and not the LEAP fund.
BioNEST Bioincubators (LEAP fund partners) will be given up to ₹5 crore fund/ partner for investing in potential startup against equity and equity linked instruments.
schemes.howToApply: Apply on: https://birac.nic.in/leapFundNew.php
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹1 crore per startup

schemes.name: Social Innovation Programme for Products Affordable & Relevant to Social Health
schemes.tagline: Grant-in-aid of up to ₹50 lakh over 18 months to build affordable healthcare products for underserved communities.
schemes.whatIsThis: The SPARSH programme supports innovators in developing affordable health-related solutions so that essential healthcare products can reach and benefit underserved communities.
schemes.objectives:
SPARSH, the social innovation initiative designed biotechnological interventions to address critical societal issues.
SPARSH revolves around nurturing "Social Innovators" within the biotech domain, equipped to identify community needs and gaps, and formulate innovative product developments or services to bridge these disparities.
eligibility: There are two components under the initiative – (i) Affordable Product Development and (ii) Social Innovation Immersion Program (SIIP). Startups and entrepreneurs are eligible for Category A under Component I. Specific eligibility criteria include the following:
Biotechnology Indian startups (less than 3 years old)
Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) – less than 3 years old
Indian academic scientists, researchers etc.
benefits: Grant-in-aid assistance up to ₹50 lakh for a period of up to 18 months.
schemes.howToApply:
The call for Letter of Intent (LoI) is announced twice a year on a thematic area with high societal impact
The LoI is to be submitted online on the BIRAC website: https://www.birac.nic.in/
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹50 lakh, subject to call guidelines

schemes.name: Agri Fund for Startups & Rural Enterprises
schemes.tagline: A ₹750 crore blended fund investing up to ₹25 crore per startup in agriculture and rural enterprises, plus incubation support.
schemes.whatIsThis: The AgriSURE Fund supports agri-startups and rural enterprises with funding and resources so they can develop innovative farming and rural solutions, improving livelihoods and strengthening the agricultural sector.
schemes.objectives:
With a focus on technology-driven, high-risk, high-impact ventures, AgriSURE is designed to fuel growth and foster innovation in the agricultural and rural startup ecosystem. A blended capital fund of ₹750 crore with SEBI Registered Category II, Alternative Investment Fund (AIF), contributions from the Government of India is ₹250 crore, NABARD is ₹250 crore, and ₹250 crore is being mobilized from banks, insurance companies, and private investors.
Aims to support innovative, technology-driven, high-impact startups in agriculture and allied sectors, improve the farm produce value chain, including support for FPOs/FPCs, farm mechanisation, IT-based solutions and rural enterprises, attract more investment into the agri and rural startup ecosystem through AIFs and direct investments, and create employment opportunities and encourage youth entrepreneurship in agriculture.
eligibility: Startups working in agriculture and rural development sectors.
SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), sector-specific, sector-agnostic and debt AIFs that invest in agri/rural startups
benefits: Investment through two routes: Fund of Funds Scheme with ₹450 crore corpus and Direct Scheme with ₹300 crore corpus.
Supports around 85 startups, with ticket size up to ₹25 crore, subject to investment merit and fund guidelines.
Provides grant-in-aid, incubation and mentoring support to eligible agri startups.
schemes.howToApply: Eligible startups or AIFs can approach NABVENTURES. https://nabventures.in/agrisure.aspx
schemes.howMuch: Fund corpus ₹750 crore; direct equity up to ₹25 crore per startup

schemes.name: Innovation & Agri-Entrepreneurship Program
schemes.tagline: Pre-seed grants up to ₹5 lakh and seed grants up to ₹25 lakh with incubation and mentoring for DPIIT-recognised agri-startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Innovation & Agri-Entrepreneurship Program (RKVY) supports agri-startups with funding, mentorship, and incubation so they can develop innovative farming solutions and strengthen the agricultural sector.
schemes.objectives:
To promote innovation and agripreneurship by providing financial support and nurturing the incubation ecosystem.
To strengthen infrastructure in agriculture and allied areas.
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups.
benefits: Pre-seed support up to ₹5 lakh and seed-stage support up to ₹25 lakh via R-ABIs.
Provides grant-in-aid, incubation and mentoring support to eligible agri startups.
schemes.howToApply: Visit https://agristartup.gov.in/ to apply
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹5 lakh (pre-seed); up to ₹25 lakh (seed stage)

schemes.name: Startup Accelerators of MeitY for Product Innovation, Development & Growth
schemes.tagline: Up to ₹40 lakh investment plus equal matching private funding and acceleration services to scale your IT product startup.
schemes.whatIsThis: SAMRIDH is a government program that supports startups by funding and accelerating innovative tech products so they can grow faster and succeed in the market.
schemes.objectives:
The SAMRIDH scheme supports existing and upcoming Accelerators to select and accelerate potential IT-based startups to scale.
The programme focuses on accelerating the startup by providing customer connect, investor connect, and internationalisation connect services.
The Scheme supports selected Accelerators for extending Accelerator services to startup and provide funding.
eligibility: Accelerators – registered as Section 8 company under Companies Act 2013 or as Society under Societies Registration Act (not-for-profit).
IT-based startups.
benefits: To startups: An investment of up to ₹40 lakh to the beneficiary startup (with an average of ₹30 lakh per startup per cohort). An equal matching private investment by the accelerator / investor of up to ₹40 lakh is disbursed to startups based on their current valuation and growth stage.

To Accelerators: Accelerators develop customized acceleration programs to fit the needs of each startup in the cohort and provide services ranging from customer connect, investor connect, capacity enhancement, product enhancement etc. for which they are provided a budget of ₹2 lakh per startup to a maximum of ₹20 lakh per cohort.
schemes.howToApply: Startups to refer to the MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) to access the list of incubators and accelerators in India benefitting from the SAMRIDH scheme and gauge the accelerators most suitable to their needs.
schemes.howMuch: Matching funding up to ₹40 lakh per startup via selected accelerators

schemes.name: Gen-Next Support for Innovative Startups
schemes.tagline: Funding from ₹10 lakh up to ₹1 crore plus mentoring and infrastructure for tech startups in Tier-II and Tier-III cities.
schemes.whatIsThis: GENESIS is a government scheme that helps early-stage startups turn innovative ideas into viable businesses by providing funding, support, and guidance so new ventures can grow and create economic value.
schemes.objectives: The Gen-Next Support for Innovative Startups (GENESIS) supports eligible Implementing Agencies (IAs) to provide funding, mentor access, and access to innovation infrastructure and ecosystem to technology startups in Tier-II and Tier-III cities in India.
eligibility: Entities registered under Section 8, Incubators, Accelerators, Educational Institutes recognised by UGC/AICTE/Formed under Central/State Act and other startups ecosystem entities that are engaged in supporting startups.
benefits: Recurring financial support will be offered to approximately 50 IAs for the duration of the scheme.
Investment and pilot funding support to startups ranging from ₹10 lakh (for early-stage startups), to up to ₹1 crore (deep-tech startups).
Capacity building initiatives for the startup ecosystem in Tier II and III cities.
schemes.howToApply:
Applications are accepted throughout the year.
Interested applicants may submit their applications online via https://msh.meity.gov.in/schemes/genesis
schemes.howMuch: Scheme outlay ₹490 crore; startup-level support varies by component

schemes.name: STPI – Next Generation Incubation Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY) / STPI
schemes.tagline: Seed funding up to ₹25 lakh plus incubation, labs, and mentoring for software-product startups at STPI centres in Tier-II/III cities.
schemes.whatIsThis: NGIS is a government scheme that helps nurture early-stage tech startups by providing incubation support, funding, and mentorship so they can develop strong, scalable business ideas.
schemes.objectives: It is a startup incubation scheme by Software Technology Parks of India (STPI) under MeitY, aimed at strengthening India’s software product startup ecosystem, especially in Tier-II/III cities. It aims to handhold 300 tech startups across 12 STPI incubation locations in Tier-II/III cities.
eligibility: Indian startups working on software product development / technology-based solutions.

DPIIT-recognised startups are encouraged; individuals, academicians, researchers, entrepreneurs, LLPs, and partnership firms may also participate but may need to register as a private limited company if selected.
benefits: Seed / risk funding up to ₹25 lakh for promising startups.
Access to state-of-the-art incubation infrastructure, labs, equipment, and testing facilities.
Mentoring, startup-connect, industry networking, marketing support, and product lifecycle guidance.
Internship / challenge grants for selected startups.
schemes.howToApply: Applicants submit proposals online through the STPI/NGIS application portal during open calls.
schemes.howMuch: Scheme outlay ₹95.03 crore; support varies per startup/city

schemes.name: Chips to Startup Programme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
schemes.tagline: Financial and technical support, EDA tools, and fab access for chip-design projects by institutions, startups, and MSMEs.
schemes.whatIsThis: The program is to build India’s semiconductor/chip-design ecosystem by training skilled manpower and supporting chip development.
schemes.objectives: It aims to train specialised professionals in VLSI and Embedded System Design over 5 years, promote design and development of ASICs, SoCs, FPGAs, reusable IP cores, and prototypes, and create a strong fabless chip-design startup ecosystem in India and support indigenous semiconductor innovation.
eligibility: Academic institutions and R&D organisations across India, including IITs, NITs, IIITs, government/private colleges, and R&D bodies.
Startups and MSMEs can participate through academia-industry collaborative projects, grand challenges, hackathons, or RFPs for System/SoC/IP Core development.
benefits: Financial and technical support for chip-design projects under approved categories.
Access to chip-design ecosystem support such as EDA tools, IP repository, fabrication/MPW support, prototyping, and mentoring.
Support for manpower training, R&D, hardware IP design, system design, prototype development, and deployment.
schemes.howToApply: Applicants submit project proposals online through the C2S portal in the prescribed MeitY provided format.
schemes.howMuch: Project/institution-based; support varies under approved C2S project

schemes.name: Innovations for Defence Excellence
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Defence / Defence Innovation Organisation (DIO)
schemes.tagline: Defence innovation grants of ₹1.5–10 crore plus incubator support for startups and MSMEs solving military challenges.
schemes.whatIsThis: iDEX is a government initiative that supports startups and innovators in building defence solutions so the military can access advanced, homegrown technologies and reduce reliance on imports.
schemes.objectives:
Innovations for Defence Excellence (iDEX) is the operational framework of the Defence Innovation Organization (DIO), a special purpose vehicle under the aegis of the Department of Defence Production, Ministry of Defence.
The iDEX framework was launched in 2018 to support the attainment of self-reliance in the fields of defence and defence production.
iDEX partners with incubators, i.e. Partner Incubators (PIs) to facilitate mentoring and access to infrastructure/test facilities to innovators, including amongst the smaller enterprises, startups and MSMES, to foster innovation in the defense sector in an integrated manner.
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups.
Any company incorporated under the Companies Act, primarily a MSME as defined under the MSME Act 2016.
Individual innovators can also apply.
benefits: Beneficiaries are provided grants/funding and other support to carry out R&D development.
Innovation activities like accelerator programs, challenges etc., are carried under the initiative, by the iDEX Partner Incubators.
Grants ranging from ₹1.5 crore to ₹10 crore awarded to startups based on the scheme/challenge applied to.
schemes.howToApply: Visit https://idex.gov.in/challenges for more information on Challenges organized under iDEX.
schemes.howMuch: SPARK grants up to ₹1.5 crore; iDEX Prime up to ₹10 crore

schemes.name: Acing Development of Innovative Technologies with iDEX
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Defence / Defence Innovation Organisation (DIO)
schemes.tagline: Grants up to ₹25 crore (50% of product budget) for startups building strategic defence and aerospace technologies via iDEX challenges.
schemes.whatIsThis: ADITI is a government program that supports startups and innovators in building new defence and aerospace technologies so India can become more self-reliant and technologically advanced.
schemes.objectives:
The ADITI Scheme aims to facilitate rapid development of strategically critical technologies which are sensitive and innovative from the defence perspective and required by the country.
Under this, relevant Challenges/Hackathons are organized for startups to seek solutions for problem statements pertaining to the earmarked technology. Winners of the ADITI challenges are incubated by Partner Incubators (PIs).
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups.
Any Indian company incorporated under the Companies Act 1956/2013.
Individual innovators can also apply.
benefits: For Partner Incubators: The limit of payment amounting to ₹9 lakh per PI, based on facilitation/completion of each milestone of their associated challenge winner.
For Startups/Innovators: Quantum of Grant up to 50% of product development budget (PDB) with maximum limit of ₹25 crore, leading to a PDB around ₹50 crore or beyond.
schemes.howToApply: Visit https://idex.gov.in/ for more information.
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹25 crore grant-in-aid per project

schemes.name: Technology Development Fund
schemes.tagline: DRDO grants covering up to 90% of a ₹10 crore project cost for Indian companies and startups developing defence technologies.
schemes.whatIsThis: Technology Development Fund (TDF) is a government scheme that gives funding and support to Indian companies and startups to develop defence technologies locally so the country can reduce dependence on imports and strengthen self-reliance.
schemes.objectives:
The TDF Scheme has been established to promote self-reliance in defence technology as a part of the 'Make in India' initiative.
The Scheme enlists requirements of Service Headquarters (SHQs) of upgrading products/ systems and futuristic defence technologies as Projects for which eligible stakeholders can apply through their platform.
eligibility: Public limited company, a private limited company, a partnership firm, a limited liability partnership, one-person company, sole proprietorship registered as per applicable Indian laws.
DPIIT recognised startups that should be incubated at one of the Central/State Government assisted incubators.
At least 50% of the shares of the startup must be owned and controlled by Indian citizens.
benefits: The funding will be through provision of grants.
Project cost of up to ₹10 crore will be considered for funding, subject to a maximum funding of 90%.
Aims to provide financial assistance to startups for prototype development and trials.
schemes.howToApply: Application process for the TDF is digitized. Applicants can register on the TDF portal - https://tdf.drdo.gov.in/
schemes.maxFunding: Up to 90% of ₹10 crore project cost
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹10 crore per project, generally up to 90% of project cost

schemes.name: IN-SPACe Seed Fund Scheme
schemes.tagline: Grants of ₹1 crore plus mentoring for DPIIT-recognised early-stage space startups moving ideas from concept to prototype.
schemes.whatIsThis: IN-SPACe Seed Fund Scheme helps early-stage space startups build and test new space-tech ideas by giving them grant funding, mentoring, and support to move from concept to prototype.
schemes.objectives: The Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre (IN-SPACe) has been formed under the Department of Space (DoS) to promote, enable, authorize and supervise non-government entities to undertake space activities.
eligibility: DPIIT recognised early-stage startups with innovative ideas and projects that use space technology to address challenges in space sector
Startups should not have received more than ₹50 lakh of monetary support under any Central or State Government Scheme.
benefits: Funding, mentorship, training and networking opportunities.
Grant of ₹1 crore provided to selected startups on milestone-basis.
schemes.howToApply: Announcement of opportunities made from time to time on https://www.inspace.gov.in/inspace?id=announcement_for_opportunities
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹1 crore per startup / NGE

schemes.name: Digital Communication Innovation Square
schemes.tagline: Milestone-based telecom R&D funding — up to ₹50 lakh for startups, ₹2 crore for MSMEs, ₹10 crore for bigger technology products.
schemes.whatIsThis: Digital Communication Innovation Square (DCIS) supports startups and innovators in developing new digital communication technologies so India can strengthen its telecom sector and drive self-reliance.
schemes.objectives: To promote the ecosystem for research, design, development, proof of concept testing, Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) creation, pilot project and manufacturing i.e. complete value chain to make India a global hub for production of telecommunication equipment and a centre for digital communication services.
eligibility: DPIIT recognised startups

Any Indian company incorporated under the Companies Act 1956/2013, primarily a Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) as defined in the MSME Act, 2006 having over 51% stakes by the Indian Citizen / NRI / OCI and Headquarter in India.

Individual Innovators will also be encouraged to apply (research & academic institutions can use this category to apply). However, release of fund shall be subjected to compliance of eligibility condition for either Startup or MSME before agreement signing.
benefits: Milestone based support:
Startups up to ₹50 lakh
MSMEs up to ₹2 crore
Technology products requiring higher funding up to ₹10 crore
schemes.howToApply: Apply on: https://dcis.dot.gov.in/
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹50 lakh per selected proposal for startups

schemes.name: Grant for Research & Entrepreneurship across Aspiring Innovators in Technical Textiles
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Textiles (National Technical Textiles Mission)
schemes.tagline: Grant-in-aid up to ₹50 lakh plus incubator support to turn technical-textile prototypes into market-ready products.
schemes.whatIsThis: GREAT gives funding and incubator support to innovators and startups in technical textiles so they can turn prototypes into market-ready products and technologies.
schemes.objectives: A scheme under the National Technical Textiles Mission (NTTM) to support technical-textile innovators and startups. It help young innovators, entrepreneurs and startups convert technical-textile prototypes into market-ready products/technologies.
eligibility: Individual innovators who are Indian citizens aged 21 years or above, incubated with an eligible incubator, and working on technical-textile innovation.
Startup companies incorporated as a private limited company, partnership firm or LLP, with turnover below ₹100 crore, not older than 5 years, and at least 51% Indian resident ownership/control.

Projects must relate to technical-textile areas such as agro-textiles, geo-textiles, medical textiles, protective textiles, smart textiles, high-performance fibres, sustainable textiles, composites, or indigenous machinery/equipment.
benefits: Grant-in-aid of normally up to ₹50 lakh per approved proposal for up to 18 months.
Funding is released milestone-wise, generally in 40:40:20 instalments.
Associated incubators receive an additional 10% of the sanctioned Government grant share for mentoring, due diligence and monitoring support.
Intellectual property generated remains with the innovator/startup or may be jointly shared with the incubator as per their agreement.
schemes.howToApply: Apply online through the GREAT portal when applications are invited by NTTM/Ministry of Textiles. https://nttm.texmin.gov.in/StartupRegistration/StartupUserRegistration
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹50 lakh for up to 18 months

schemes.name: Bharat’s Hub for Agritech, Resilience, Advancement & Incubation for Export Innovation
schemes.tagline: APEDA acceleration, market access, and ecosystem connections to make agri-food and agritech startups export-ready.
schemes.whatIsThis: BHARATI helps agri-food and agritech startups become export-ready by giving them acceleration support, market access, and connections to the ecosystem needed to sell innovative products globally.
schemes.objectives:
It is a flagship initiative of Agricultural & Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA) to strengthen India’s agri-food and processed food export ecosystem through innovation and startup acceleration.
It aims to empower agri-food and agri-tech startups and create new export opportunities, promote innovation in high-value agri-food products, export technologies, packaging, traceability, logistics, sustainability, and quality assurance.
eligibility: Agri-food startups developing innovative/high-value products for export, technology and service startups offering export-enabling solutions across the value chain.
Solution providers for SPS challenges, including startups, individual researchers, innovators and solo/self-employed entrepreneurs.
APEDA-registered exporters seeking to innovate or expand high-value product portfolios for international markets.
Farmer Producer Companies (FPCs) developing innovative/high-value agri-food products or SPS-related solutions.
benefits: Acceleration support covering product development, export readiness, regulatory compliance and market access.
Access to APEDA’s ecosystem, including industry bodies, state agricultural boards, universities, IITs/NITs and accelerators.
Support for innovative categories such as GI-tagged products, organic foods, superfoods, processed Indian foods, livestock products and AYUSH products.
schemes.howToApply: Visit https://apeda.gov.in/bharati/ for more information.
schemes.howMuch: Pilot cohort supports 100 startups; no fixed grant disclosed

schemes.name: Promotion of Research & Innovation in Mining, Minerals & Recycling Sector
schemes.tagline: Milestone-based grants up to ₹2 crore for early-stage mining, minerals, metals, and recycling startups to build prototypes and pilots.
schemes.whatIsThis: S&T-PRISM helps early-stage startups in mining, minerals, metals, and recycling turn innovative ideas into working prototypes and pilot-ready solutions through funding and incubation support.
schemes.objectives: The S&T-PRISM program aims to promote the ecosystem for research, design, development, proof of concept testing, IPR creation, pilot project and manufacturing i.e., complete value chain in mining, mineral processing, metallurgy and recycling sector. There are two components in the program: Startup Funding and Setting up Incubation Centres.
eligibility: Startups operating at the early stages in the mining and metal industry.
benefits: To Startups: Grant of up to ₹2 crore on milestone basis to startups showing capability, intent, and promise to be able to produce functional prototypes or to productize existing technologies.
To Incubators: Grant of up to ₹10 crore to incubators disbursed on milestone-basis.
schemes.howToApply: Follow the instructions provided in the scheme guidelines https://mines.gov.in/admin/storage/app/uploads/655212bdb9e5b1699877565.pdf
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹2 crore per approved project

schemes.name: NIDHI – Entrepreneur in Residence
schemes.tagline: Monthly fellowship of ₹10,000–₹30,000 for up to 18 months, plus co-working space and mentoring to turn your startup idea into a business.
schemes.whatIsThis: NIDHI–EIR is a fellowship scheme that gives young people with startup ideas monthly financial support, workspace, and mentoring so they can turn their idea into a business
schemes.objectives: The initiative NIDHI – Entrepreneur in Residence (EIR) is executed at selected NIDHI-Technology Business Incubator (TBI) known as Programme Executing Partner (PEP). These approved TBIs then select entrepreneurs under the programme pursuing a promising technology business idea. The objective is to encourage graduating students to take to entrepreneurship by providing support as a fellowship.
eligibility: The entrepreneur should be an Indian citizen. They should have completed minimum 4 years of formal full time undergraduate/post graduate education or 3 years degree or diploma program and have 2-year full time work experience post degree or diploma.
benefits: Grant ranging from ₹10,000 to ₹30,000 per month for a period of 12 months, extendable up to 18 months.
The selected NIDHI-TBI will received a grant of ₹39.6 lakh each year, of which ₹36 lakh is to be provided to the NIDHI EIRs.
Co-working spaces for developing the idea into a marketable product.
Guidance from experienced, innovative and highly successful entrepreneurs on the business concept, strategy or venture and insight into specific industries or markets.
schemes.howToApply: The program is announced by the NIDHI-TBI locally. Applications by the EIR are to be submitted in a prescribed format
schemes.maxFunding: Up to ₹30,000 per month for 18 months
schemes.howMuch: ₹10,000–₹30,000 per month for up to 12 months

schemes.name: National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems – Technology Innovation Hubs
schemes.tagline: 25 Technology Innovation Hubs give startups, students, and researchers funding and non-financial support to build advanced-tech products.
schemes.whatIsThis: NM-ICPS Technology Innovation Hubs support people and startups working on advanced technologies by giving them funding and non-financial support to turn ideas into real products and businesses.
schemes.objectives: The Department of Science & Technology (DST) is implementing multi-stakeholder National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber Physical System (NM-ICPS). The Mission works with all the concerned Ministries/Departments to identify their technology needs, develop solutions and technical support. It aims at development of technology platforms to carry out R&D, Translational Research, Product Development, Incubating & Supporting Startups as well as Commercialization. Under NM-ICPS, 25 Technology Innovation Hubs (TIH) have been set up across the country.
eligibility: Under the 25 TIHs, individuals and entities including startups, students, entrepreneurs, and researchers, are invited to apply.
benefits: Eligible beneficiaries are provided with financial and non-financial support for research and development.
schemes.howToApply: Technology Innovation Hubs release call for proposals periodically for different projects. Interested individuals/entities are advised to regularly check the call for proposals section on the NM-ICPS website.
schemes.howMuch: Mission outlay ₹3,660 crore; startup support varies by TIH/call

schemes.name: National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations - Technology Business Incubator
schemes.tagline: Grant support for institutions to set up Technology Business Incubators, covering capital and recurring costs of running the incubator.
schemes.whatIsThis: NIDHI-TBI supports institutions in setting up incubators so they can help technology-based ideas grow into startups.
schemes.objectives: The initiative aims to convert technology-based innovations into startups by supporting institutions in setting up Technology Business Incubators (TBIs). The initiative provides support for non-recurring and recurring expenses of TBIs.
eligibility: Host Institute (HI) such as Academic/Technical/R&D Institutes and other institutions with a proven track record in promotion of technology-based entrepreneurship, with adequate expertise and infrastructure to support incubation activities, or

A legal entity registered in India with the clear purpose and objective of promoting research, innovation, and entrepreneurial ecosystem. Not-for-profit legal entity registered as a society/trust/section 8 company
benefits: Non-recurring capital: For publicly funded institutes, 100% support can be provided for renovation/furnishing. In case of other institutes, certain percentage of the total cost (excluding land & building) will be provided.
Recurring: Out of the total recurring expenditure incurred by the NIDHI-TBI against the approved amount, following mode of funding by DST would be followed on actual recurring expenditure.
schemes.howToApply: Proposal is to be submitted in the prescribed proforma available at https://nidhi.dst.gov.in/
schemes.howMuch: Incubator-level support varies; generally multi-crore per DST approval

schemes.name: NIDHI - Inclusive-Technology Business Incubator
schemes.tagline: Up to ₹5 crore over 3 years for institutions in underserved regions to set up inclusive incubators hosting 30+ local startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: NIDHI i-TBI supports institutions in underserved regions to set up incubators so local innovators and startups can get the space, funding, and support needed to build new ventures.
schemes.objectives: The NIDHI i-TBI initiative aims to support institutions in regions where innovation and startup incubation ecosystem is at nascent stages and ‘Inclusive TBI’ can play a vital role in normalizing resources and value creation across the country. The i-TBI would create a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship in the Host Institute (HI) and nearby geographies.
eligibility: The HI is to provide up to 10,000 square feet (minimum 7,000 square feet) of built-up space to host i-TBI in the campus.

The HI shall agree to establish separate Section 8 company to professionally manage the i-TBI and will execute a lease agreement between HI and new (Section 8) entity, to lease the space earmarked for the i-TBI to new entity at no cost or at minimum rate for 10 years or beyond.
The i-TBI should be capable of accommodating at least 30 innovative ventures/startups.
benefits: DST will provide the necessary capital fund to establish the infrastructure of i-TBI.
The i-TBI is provided with a maximum funding of ₹5 crore over a period of 3 years.
Funding for non-recurring expenditure:
For private institutions – 50:50 share between DST and HI
For public institutions – 75:25 share between DST and HI, respectively.
The ignition grant shall be for development of prototype/products.
schemes.howToApply: Visit the online portal i.e., https://nidhi.dst.gov.in/
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹5 crore over 3 years to incubator

schemes.name: NIDHI-Accelerator
schemes.tagline: Up to ₹40 lakh per cohort for DST-backed incubators to run accelerator programs with mentoring, networking, and demo-day awards.
schemes.whatIsThis: NIDHI-Accelerator helps incubators run short, intensive programs that give promising startups mentoring, networks, and demo-day support so they can grow faster.
schemes.objectives:
The umbrella programme ‘National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations (NIDHI)’ developed in 2016 aims to nurture the innovation ecosystem in India. Key components of the NIDHI program are outlined in the following sections.
NIDHI Accelerators are positioned as post-incubation initiatives linked with the existing incubators to supplement, complement the scaling up of the startup value chain. Under the programme, DST encourages its existing TBIs and other incubators to extensively adopt and evolve the accelerator mode. It aims to fast track the growth of potential startups through rigorous mentoring and networking support in a short span through existing TBIs.
eligibility: TBIs previously funded or currently funded by DST or any other Central or State government with a minimum 3 years in existence, having nurtured and incubated at least 20 startups in multiple domains.
benefits: Contribution towards the overall budget for running accelerators shall not exceed ₹40 lakh per program of one cohort. It is further divided into two categories:
Accelerator Program with a maximum limit of ₹30 lakh, that includes manpower, logistics, travel, mentoring, infrastructure, training, and all other cost.
Demo Day Award with maximum budget of ₹10 lakh where top 3 to 5 startups of the batch are awarded on the basis of their performance/achievements during the accelerator program.
schemes.howToApply: Visit the online portal https://nidhi.dst.gov.in/
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹40 lakh (₹30 lakh to accelerator; ₹10 lakh to startups)

schemes.name: NIDHI-Centres of Excellence
schemes.tagline: Gives established incubators up to ₹50 crore over 5 years to build world-class facilities for high-potential tech startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: NIDHI-CoE supports established incubators in building world-class facilities and services so that high-potential technology startups can turn innovations into scalable businesses.
schemes.objectives: Under NIDHI-Centres of Excellence (CoE), the DST empowers incubators to provide an enabling environment for the growth of technology startups to enhance the prospects of their success. The NIDHI-CoE proposes to strengthen existing capacities of STEP/TBIs and support potential startups with different pursuits and practices in transferring technological innovations into marketable products/high-growth companies. DST has priority areas for supporting NIDHI-CoEs.
eligibility: Existing incubator with incubation experience of at least 5 years.
For non-academic institution, it should be a legal entity registered in India with the clear purpose of promoting research, innovation, and entrepreneurial ecosystem.
The financial support for establishing a NIDHI-CoE would be extended to a not-for-profit legal entity registered as a Society/Trust/Section 8 company.
The host institution should be in existence for at least 8 years. This will not apply to entities created by the Central or State governments.
benefits: An enhanced support of maximum of ₹50 crore in 5 years.
Public funded institutions: Would be eligible for 100% support including building
Private institution: Eligible for recurring support of ₹20 crore and a maximum of ₹1 crore for equipment

The support would include design/prototype development & Validation Centres along with the Patent Facilitation centres, state-of-the-art technology based TBI facilities to accommodate minimum 100 startups and an accelerator for 10 high potential startups.
schemes.howToApply: Proposals are received throughout the year. Format for the proposal is provided on the portal https://nidhi.dst.gov.in/
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹50 crore over 5 years to incubator

schemes.name: BioAngels
schemes.tagline: Connects biotech and related startups with angel investors who bring funding, deep domain mentoring, and industry access.
schemes.whatIsThis: BioAngels helps biotech and related startups raise investment by connecting them with angel investors who also provide expert mentoring and industry access.
schemes.objectives: BioAngels is a unique partnership between BIRAC, an enterprise of the Department of Biotechnology, and IAN, India’s single largest horizontal platform for seed and early-stage investing. It is focused on supporting Biotech, Medtech, Healthtech, Pharma, Agritech & Cleantech startups to raise their angel round from Angel Investors who bring deep domain expertise.
eligibility: Startups and entrepreneurs operating in Biotech, Medtech, Healthtech, Pharma, Cleantech & Agritech sectors.
benefits: BioAngels provides financial incentives, mentoring, and market access.
It aims to fuel the ecosystem through interactions with high-quality investors and industry leaders, whilst emphasizing a strong operational focus, frameworks, processes, and governance.
The inclusive group of ecosystem stakeholders at BioAngels comprises HNIs, angel investors, family offices, strategic investors, corporates, and VCs.
schemes.howToApply: Apply here: https://bioangels.vc/
schemes.howMuch: Varies by angel round / investor decision

schemes.name: BioNEST Programme
schemes.ministry: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), Department of Biotechnology
schemes.tagline: Grant-in-aid for institutions and incubators to build bio-incubation facilities with shared labs and equipment for biotech startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: The BioNEST program enables certain entities (educational institutions, incubators etc.) to create globally competent bio-incubation facilities for targeted scaling up of biotech startup ecosystem.
schemes.objectives:
The BioNEST (Bioincubators Nurturing Entrepreneurship for Scaling Technologies) program is supported by BIRAC to create globally competent bio-incubation facilities across the country in order to facilitate the targeted scaling up of biotech startup ecosystem.
To provide entrepreneurs with incubation space with shared access to high end infrastructure, specialized and advanced equipment
To provide entrepreneurs with business mentorship, IP, egal and regulatory guidance, and networking opportunities.
eligibility: Relevant incorporated Indian legal entities including: Educational institutions, Business Incubators, Biotech-Parks, State government Science and Technology bodies, Biotech State Councils, Research Hospitals.
benefits: Grant-in-aid, based on the need and scope of the project as adjudged by the BioNEST committee.
BIRAC funding to cater to refurbishment/ renovation of space, equipment, furniture and operational cost.
schemes.howToApply: Interested applicants may submit their applications online via https://www.birac.nic.in/desc_new.php?id=1120.
schemes.howMuch: Incubator-level grant varies by call; startup support indirect

schemes.name: Biotechnology Industry Partnership Programme
schemes.ministry: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), Department of Biotechnology
schemes.tagline: Cost-sharing R&D support with industry; first-time BIRAC applicants can get a one-time grant-in-aid of up to ₹50 lakh.
schemes.whatIsThis: Under BIPP, the Government offers support to startups in partnership with industries for research in frontier futuristic technology areas.
schemes.objectives: The Biotechnology Industry Partnership Programme (BIPP) is a government partnership with industries for support on a cost sharing basis for research in frontier futuristic technology areas. It is focused on IP creation with ownership retained by Indian industry and wherever relevant, by collaborating scientists.
eligibility: Single company or consortia of companies incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013; or Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) incorporated under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008, jointly with or without the following:
Public/private university/colleges in India
National research laboratories
Not-for-profit private research labs/societies/foundation/NGO
benefits: One time grant-in-aid (subject to a maximum of ₹50 lakh) is provided by BIRAC to those startups which have not received grant-in-aid under any scheme of BIRAC in past.

If the startup has received funding from BIRAC before, BIRAC contribution will be restricted to a maximum of 70% of the total budget of the respective entity (Minimum 30% of the total budget will be contributed by the respective Company.
schemes.howToApply:
Call for Applications are announced twice a year (1st April – 15th May; 1st September – 15th October)
Proposals are required to be submitted online only. Call for proposals and application link can be found on the BIRAC website: https://birac.nic.in/cfp.php
schemes.howMuch: Project-based; varies by approved cost and scheme terms

schemes.name: Empowering Youth for Undertaking Value Added Innovative Translational Research
schemes.ministry: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), Department of Biotechnology
schemes.tagline: Gives students and young researchers fellowships of ₹30,000-₹50,000 a month plus research grants up to ₹5 lakh for biotech ideas.
schemes.whatIsThis: E-YUVA supports students and young researchers with funding, mentoring, and incubation support so they can turn useful biotech ideas into real solutions or startups.
schemes.objectives: E-YUVA was initiated by BIRAC to promote a culture of applied research and need-oriented (societal or industry) entrepreneurial innovation among young students and researchers. It is implemented through dedicated hubs called E-Yuva Centers (EYCs), housed within the University/Institute set up and mentored by a BIRAC BioNEST supported bio-incubator. The Scheme provides support under two categories: BIRAC’s Innovation Fellows (post-graduates and above), and BIRAC’s E-YUVA Fellows (undergraduate students).
eligibility: Innovation Fellows: An Indian Student who has completed Masters/ Ph.D. in any discipline.
E-YUVA Fellows: Indian students pursuing under graduation in any domain.
benefits: The Scheme provides funding support – through fellowship and research grants.
Monthly fellowship grant provided under the Scheme ranges between ₹30,000-₹50,000. Annual research grant range between ₹3 lakh to ₹5 lakh.
Technical and business mentoring, exposure to bioincubation model, orientation to entrepreneurial culture etc. is also provided to students.
schemes.howToApply:
Two Calls for Applications announced in a year.
Online applications are to be submitted through the BIRAC website: https://birac.nic.in
The shortlisted applicants/teams are called for a presentation before a Technical Expert Committee along with BIRAC nominee at respective EYCs
schemes.maxFunding: Fellowship ₹30,000-₹50,000/month + research grant up to ₹5 lakh/year
schemes.howMuch: Commonly fellowship plus innovation support up to ₹5 lakh per project

schemes.name: Students Innovations for Translation & Advancement of Research Explorations
schemes.tagline: Grants up to ₹15 lakh plus training and mentoring for biotech student innovators to turn research ideas into startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: SITARE supports student innovators in biotechnology with funding, training, and mentoring so they can turn promising research ideas into practical solutions or startups.
schemes.objectives:
Students Innovations for Translation & Advancement of Research Explorations (SITARE) Scheme aims to promote and support innovative student projects in the area of biotechnology.
The scheme has two components, SITARE-Gandhian Young Technological Innovation Award Grant (SITARE-GYTI), and SITARE-Appreciation Grant.
eligibility: SITARE-GYTI: An Indian Student pursuing Ph.D./Masters in any discipline.
SITARE-Appreciation Grant: Students pursuing undergraduate programme in any discipline, preferably from Tier II/Tier III cities and aspirational districts within India.
benefits: SITARE-GYTI: Innovative student projects receive funding support of up to ₹15 lakh each for projects with commercial potential leading to creation of biotech startups.

SITARE-Appreciation Grant: Students selected to attend Biotech Innovation Ignition School (BIIS) workshops are eligible to receive funding support of up to ₹1 lakh each, followed by training and mentoring.
schemes.howToApply:
For SITARE-GYTI: The call for application opens once a year in August/September for 45-60 days. Proposals shall be submitted online only.
SITARE-Appreciation Grant: Applications need to be submitted online only. Call for applications are invited against advertisement by SITARE Partner
schemes.howMuch: Translational support commonly up to ₹15 lakh, subject to call

schemes.name: Promoting Academic Research Conversion to Enterprise
schemes.tagline: BIRAC grants for academia to develop biotech proofs-of-concept and validate them with industry partners — up to ₹50 lakh under AIR.
schemes.whatIsThis: PACE helps academic institutions turn important research into early-stage products and then supports industry partnerships to validate and take those innovations closer to real-world use.
schemes.objectives: The PACE (Promoting Academic Research Conversion to Enterprise) encourages academia to develop technology/product (up to PoC stage) of societal/national importance (under Academic Innovation Research or AIR), and its subsequent validation by an industrial partner (under Contract Research Scheme or CRS.
eligibility: For AIR: Academia (Public or Private Institute, University, NGO, or Research Foundation) with a well-established support system for research shall be the primary applicant. Collaboration with Industry/LLP is optional in this component.
For CRS: Mandatory collaboration with Industry/LLP is needed. The Industry collaborator must have a minimum of 51% of the shares of the Company held by Indian Citizens holding Indian passport.
benefits: PACE provides for BIRAC contribution of up to 100% of the project cost.
Financial support under AIR: Total cost of the project must not exceed ₹50 lakh (non-recurring cost must not exceed 10% of the total cost)
Financial support under CRS: Funding in the form of grant is provided to both academic as well as industrial partners. There is no ceiling to the funding under CRS.
schemes.howToApply: Call for Applications are announced twice a year (1st April – 15th May; and 1st September – 15th October). Besides two regular calls, BIRAC may also announce need-based additional calls whose terms and conditions would be worked out separately and described in their respective RFPs.
schemes.maxFunding: Up to ₹50 lakh (AIR); no ceiling under CRS
schemes.howMuch: Project-based; commonly up to ₹50 lakh–₹1 crore depending on PACE component

schemes.name: Small Business Innovation Research Initiative
schemes.tagline: 100% BIRAC grant-in-aid up to ₹50 lakh (partial above that) for biotech startups developing and validating products with partners.
schemes.whatIsThis: SBIRI supports biotech startups by funding product and process innovation, often with research partners, so they can develop and validate ideas for commercial use.
schemes.objectives:
Small Business Innovation Research Initiative (SBIRI) aims to boost innovation focused Public-Private Partnership (PPP) approach in the area of Biotechnology.
SBIRI creates an enabling platform for the creation of product and process development and their commercialisation.
It facilitates innovation by enabling collaboration between startups, industry, public institutions, and the Government to promote research and innovation in the Indian biotechnology sector.
eligibility: Biotechnology startups with/without partnerships with public/private universities, public or private research institutions, or societies/foundation/NGO.
benefits: For a project cost of up to ₹50 lakh, 100% BIRAC grant-in-aid is given to select applicants and collaboration company (if any)
For a project cost over ₹50 lakh, BIRAC grant-in-aid would be ₹50 lakh along with 70% of the project cost over and above ₹50 lakh. The remaining cost is to be borne by the applicant.
schemes.howToApply:
Call for Applications are announced twice a year (1st April – 15th May; and 1st September - 15th October).
Besides two regular calls, BIRAC may also announce need-based additional calls whose terms and conditions would be worked out separately and described in their respective RFPs.
schemes.maxFunding: 100% grant up to ₹50 lakh project cost
schemes.howMuch: Early-stage support commonly up to ₹50 lakh grant; larger support as per call

schemes.name: TIDE 2.0 – Technology Incubation & Development of Entrepreneurs
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
schemes.tagline: Grants from ₹4 lakh to ₹40 lakh plus incubation and mentoring for DPIIT-recognised IT startups, from idea to market-ready product.
schemes.whatIsThis: TIDE 2.0 helps early-stage tech startups get funding, incubation, and mentoring so they can turn ideas and prototypes into market-ready products.
schemes.objectives: TIDE 2.0 scheme is a fellowship/grant for aspiring entrepreneurs, aiming to create a holistic ecosystem by providing financial and technical support to technology startups by creating necessary linkages.
eligibility: Information Technology based, DPIIT-recognised startups.
benefits: Extended to Entrepreneurs-in-residence (EiR) at various stages:
Up to ₹4 lakh for idea to Proof of Concept.
Up to ₹7 lakh grants for prototype development.
Up to ₹40 lakh investment for product development and market outreach.
schemes.howToApply: Startups to refer to the MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) to access the list of incubators in India benefitting from the TIDE 2.0 scheme and gauge the incubators most suitable to their needs.
schemes.howMuch: PoC grants up to ₹7 lakh; larger support varies by centre/component

schemes.name: Design Linked Incentive Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
schemes.tagline: EDA tools, IP cores and incentives up to ₹15 crore (design) plus ₹30 crore (deployment) for Indian chip-design startups and MSMEs.
schemes.whatIsThis: DLI provides incentives and access to advanced design infrastructure for domestic startups and MSMEs.
schemes.objectives: The Design Linked Incentive (DLI) Scheme is a key instrument under the Semicon India Programme to catalyze a strong, self-reliant chip design ecosystem by providing financial incentives and access to advanced design infrastructure for domestic startups and MSMEs.
eligibility: Domestic companies, startups and MSMEs engaged in semiconductor design or semiconductor-linked design.
Target areas include ICs, chipsets, SoCs, systems, IP cores and semiconductor-linked designs.

Applicants claiming incentives must retain domestic status, i.e., more than 50% beneficial ownership by resident Indian citizens / Indian companies ultimately owned and controlled by resident Indians, for 3 years after claiming incentives.
benefits: Chip Design Infrastructure Support: Access to EDA tools, IP cores, MPW prototyping and post-silicon validation support.
Product Design Linked Incentive: Reimbursement of up to 50% of eligible expenditure, capped at ₹15 crore per application.
Deployment Linked Incentive: Incentive of 6% to 4% of net sales turnover over 5 years, capped at ₹30 crore per application.
schemes.howToApply:
Visit the official DLI portal and register/login.
Submit the online application with required project and applicant details.
Track application status online; approvals/certificates are issued electronically
schemes.maxFunding: Up to ₹15 crore (design) + ₹30 crore (deployment) per application
schemes.howMuch: Product design-linked incentive up to 50% of eligible expenditure; deployment incentive 4–6% of net sales (cap ₹30 crore)

schemes.name: Antariksh Venture Capital Fund/ Space VCF
schemes.ministry: Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre (IN-SPACe), Department of Space
schemes.tagline: A ₹1,005 crore government-anchored VC fund that makes equity investments in unlisted Indian space companies at TRL 4 and above.
schemes.whatIsThis: Antariksh Venture Capital Fund is a SEBI registered Alternative Investment Fund that invests in unlisted Indian space companies.
schemes.objectives: Antariksh Venture Capital Fund is a SEBI registered Alternative Investment Fund with the key investor being Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre (IN-SPACe), Department of Space, Government of India. The fund received registration from SEBI in October 2025 and achieved its initial closing with a committed corpus of ₹1,005 crore. The Fund’s investment objective is to provide capital to companies in the space sector at various stages of development as well as support the scaling of operations and the commercialisation of new technologies, empowering the companies to contribute to India’s broader space ambitions.
eligibility: Indian companies operating in the Space sector with Technology Readiness Level (TRL) of 4 and above.
benefits: The Fund would invest primarily by way of investments in privately negotiated equity, convertible instruments and / or other permissible instruments in unlisted Indian space companies.
schemes.howToApply:
• Interested companies can apply on the website: https://www.sidbiventure.co.in/antariksh_venture_capital_fund.html
• Application form: https://forms.cloud.microsoft/pages/responsepage.aspx?id=aRgAbgzE306qFp7_SRf-s0ExpWkb3JRCt9X0vPRtOjRUNDVJWE5JNDJJQjcyR1g1S0xDMlFFNkZMTS4u&route=shorturl.
schemes.howMuch: Target corpus ~₹1,600 crore; first close ₹1,005 crore; company ticket varies

schemes.name: Space Technology Adoption Fund
schemes.ministry: Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre (IN-SPACe), Department of Space
schemes.tagline: Funds up to 60% of project cost (capped at ₹25 crore) to help space companies turn TRL-3/4 technologies into market-ready products.
