schemes.whatIsThis: The Space Technology Adoption Fund (TAF) helps Indian space companies turn early-stage technologies into commercial products by giving funding to support development and market readiness.
schemes.objectives:
• IN-SPACe aims to foster commercialisation in space system by promoting Indian non-government entities (NGEi) to develop space products which in turn shall lead to scaling up the productionisation to meet the Global and Domestic Space Sector demands.
• As part of this endeavor, the Technology Adoption Fund (TAF), a funding scheme designed to promote innovation and drive the commercialisation of cutting-edge technologies in the space sector has been instituted with a total funding of ₹500 crore.
eligibility: Indian non-government entities (NGEi) including startups, MSMEs and large industry.
benefits: Partial funding provided to the NGEi to work towards transforming early-stage technologies (TRL-3/4) into commercially viable product (TRL-8/9). The funding would be up to 60% for Startups/MSME and 40% for large industry of the project cost. The maximum funding which can be offered for a project is capped at ₹25 crore.
schemes.howToApply: Interested companies can register and apply on the website: https://www.inspace.gov.in/inspace?id=inspace_taf
schemes.howMuch: TAF corpus ₹500 crore; project-level funding varies

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Trust Fund for Micro & Small Enterprises
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.tagline: Get collateral-free bank loans as a micro or small enterprise, with the trust guaranteeing cover of up to ₹10 crore per borrower.
schemes.whatIsThis: Under the CGTMSE, collateral free loan is provided to Micro and Small Enterprises (MSE) units.
schemes.objectives: The Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises and Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI) jointly established a Trust named Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises (CGTMSE) in order to implement Credit Guarantee Scheme for Micro and Small Enterprises. The corpus of CGTMSE is contributed by Government of India and SIDBI. 75% of the loan amount to the bank is guaranteed by the Trust Fund.
eligibility: Loans sanctioned to Micro and Small Enterprises (MSE) units, as per the definition of the Ministry of MSMEs engaged in all eligible activities including trading activity.
benefits: Collateral free loan is available for individual MSE on payment of guarantee fee to bank by the MSE

The cap of ₹10 crore is the maximum guarantee coverage limit (irrespective of the unit activity including Trading) per borrower based on the outstanding credit facilities and the borrowers can avail incremental credit facilities
Modified guarantee cover is available for Women entrepreneurs / Units located in Northeast Region.
schemes.howToApply: Apply to a MLI (Banks, NBFCs, FIs) registered with CGTMSE for MSE loan (Term Loan or Working Capital). Check the official CGTMSE website for the list of MLIs: https://www.cgtmse.in/
schemes.howMuch: Guarantee coverage ceiling up to ₹10 crore; guarantee percentage varies

schemes.name: Self-Reliant India Fund
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.tagline: Growth capital as equity or quasi-equity for MSMEs through government-backed Daughter Funds under a ₹50,000 crore provision.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Self-Reliant India Fund (SRI Fund) helps MSMEs raise equity or quasi-equity capital through government-backed investment funds.
schemes.objectives:
• The SRI fund has the provision of ₹10,000 crore from Government of India and ₹40,000 crore through Private Equity / Venture Capital Funds. In totality, it will create a provision of ₹50,000 crore equity support to MSMEs, who have the potential and viability to grow and become large units.
• SRI Fund is being implemented by NSIC Venture Capital fund Limited (NVCFL), which is an Alternative Investment Fund (AIF) of Category II registered with SEBI. SRI fund is oriented to provide the funding support through NVCFL to the Daughter Funds for onward provision to MSMEs as growth capital, in the form of equity or quasi- equity.
eligibility: MSMEs, defined as per the MSMED Act, as amended from time to time.
benefits: Provision of growth capital in the form of equity or quasi-equity through Daughter Funds.
schemes.howToApply:
• Interested MSMEs can fill the form to show their interest: https://nvcfl.co.in/Home/InterestAsMSME
• Further, NVCFL also publishes an official list of empaneled Daughter Funds, including fund names, investment managers, sector focus, contact persons, email IDs, phone numbers, and addresses, which can be used to directly approach funds aligned to respective sector/stage - https://nvcfl.co.in/Corporate/EmpanelledDaughterFunds
schemes.howMuch: Overall provision ₹50,000 crore; mother fund ₹10,000 crore; enterprise ticket varies

schemes.name: MSME Champions Scheme (erstwhile Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Technology Upgradation)
schemes.tagline: Subsidies and support for MSMEs covering ZED certification, lean manufacturing, design, IPR, incubation and technology upgradation.
schemes.whatIsThis: The MSME Champions Scheme helps MSMEs improve quality, adopt better technology, become more competitive, and grow through support for certification, innovation, design, and lean manufacturing.
schemes.objectives:
• Ministry of MSME had been implementing Credit Linked Capital Subsidy and Technology Upgradation Scheme (CLCS-TUS) for promoting competitiveness amongst Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) by the way of wastage reduction through Lean Manufacturing, support for Design improvement, building awareness on Intellectual Property Rights, Zero Defect Zero Effect (ZED) Scheme, digitally empowerment of MSME through Digital MSME and to promote adoption of latest technologies in manufacturing as well as knowledge based innovation MSMEs through Incubation across India.
• MSME Champions scheme has been formulated through Standing Finance Committee (SFC) by merging all 6 components of erstwhile Technology Upgradation Scheme (TUS) for a period of 5 years i.e., 2021-22 to 2025-26.
• There are 3 components under the new MSME Champions scheme:
  • MSME-Sustainable (ZED)
  • MSME-Competitive (Lean)
  • MSME-Innovative (for Incubation, IPR, Design)
Digital MSME (yet to be launched)
eligibility: MSMEs
benefits: Subsidy on cost of ZED certification, Financial Assistance in Testing/Quality/Product, Handholding Support, Support in Technology Upgradation for Zero Effect, MSME KAWACH (Knowledge Acquisition through WASH for an Accelerated COVID-19 Handling) Certification, Graded incentives.
schemes.howToApply: Apply here: https://champions.gov.in/Government-India/Ministry-MSME-Portal-handholding/msme-problem-complaint-welcome.htm
schemes.howMuch: Component-wise; varies under ZED, Lean and Innovative guidelines

schemes.name: International Cooperation Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.tagline: Reimburses MSMEs for participating in international trade fairs, exhibitions, and buyer-seller meets to enter export markets.
schemes.whatIsThis: The International Cooperation Scheme helps MSMEs explore export markets by supporting their participation in international trade fairs, exhibitions, buyer-seller meets, and other activities that build global market access.
schemes.objectives: This scheme aims to support MSMEs in entering the international market, by providing them opportunity to participate in international exhibitions, fair, buyer-seller market abroad. Incentives/reimbursements are offered to MSMEs for support. The objective of the Scheme is to provide opportunities to MSMEs to continuously update themselves to meet the challenges emerging out of changes in technology, changes in demand, emergence of new markets, etc.
eligibility: MSMEs, State/Central Government Organizations/Institutions and Registered Industry/Enterprise Associations etc.
benefits: The scheme provides financial assistance on reimbursement to beneficiaries.

The Scheme has envisaged three components to provide support: ‘Market Development Assistance’ and ‘Capacity Building of First Time Exporters (CBFTE)’, and Framework for International Market Intelligence Dissemination (IMID).
The ‘Capacity Building of First Time Exporters (CBFTE)’ component of the Scheme provides reimbursement to new Micro & Small Enterprises (MSE) exporters for costs incurred on certain defined exports.
schemes.howToApply: Applications can be made through the portal https://ic.msme.gov.in/IC_APP/IC_Welcome.aspx
schemes.howMuch: Assistance varies by event category and guidelines

schemes.name: National SC-ST Hub Scheme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises
schemes.tagline: Gives SC/ST entrepreneurs subsidies, training, marketing and mentoring support, and access to government procurement.
schemes.whatIsThis: The National SC-ST Hub Scheme helps SC/ST entrepreneurs start and grow businesses by providing support with funding, training, technology, marketing, and access to government procurement opportunities.
schemes.objectives: The objective of the Scheme is to provide professional support to Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe entrepreneurs to fulfill the obligations under the Central Government Public Procurement Policy for Micro and Small Enterprises Order 2012, adopt applicable business practices and leverage the Stand-Up India initiatives. The National SC-ST Hub works leverages various sub-schemes of the Government for the benefit of the SC/ST entrepreneurs.
eligibility: Existing and aspiring SC/ST entrepreneurs are the targeted beneficiaries.
benefits: Special Credit Linked Capital Subsidy for technology enablement
Capacity building of the beneficiaries
25% subsidy on purchase of plant & machinery/equipment or ₹25 lakh whichever is less.
Marketing and mentoring support through different programs, along with skills training and distribution of trade specific tool kits

Reimbursement of fees charged for bank loan processing, testing services, membership of Export Promotion Council, membership in Govt. promoted eCommerce Portals, Single Point Registration Scheme of NSI.
schemes.howToApply: The scheme is implemented through online provision developed on NSSH website. https://www.scsthub.in/
schemes.howMuch: Component-wise; direct grant amount varies

schemes.name: Atal Innovation Mission – Atal Tinkering Labs
schemes.tagline: Gives schools a ₹20 lakh grant-in-aid to set up Atal Tinkering Labs where students learn STEM and innovation skills.
schemes.whatIsThis: Atal Tinkering Laboratories (ATLs) are being established in schools across India as a workspace where students can learn innovation skills.
schemes.objectives: Atal Innovation Mission is establishing Atal Tinkering Laboratories (ATLs) in schools across India. The objective is to foster curiosity, creativity, and imagination in young minds; and inculcate skills such as design mindset, computational thinking, adaptive learning, physical computing etc. ATL is a workspace where young minds can give shape to their ideas through hands on do-it-yourself mode; and learn innovation skills. Young children will get a chance to work with tools and equipment to understand the concepts of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering and Math.
eligibility: Schools in India (having at least Grade/Class VI to X) managed by State/Union Territory/Central Government, Local body (Municipality/Nagar Nigam), Private Trusts/Society or Tribal/Social welfare Department can apply for financial support in the form of ATL Grant-in-Aid
benefits: An ATL school is eligible for grant-in-aid of ₹20 lakh, with ₹10 lakh for capital expense and remaining ₹10 lakh for operational and maintenance expenses over a period of five years
schemes.howToApply: Interested schools intending to apply for ATLs may visit the AIM website and submit their application online to the Atal Innovation Mission, NITI Aayog. https://aim.gov.in/

links.label: All details with respect to ATL

schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹20 lakh per selected school over multiple years

schemes.name: Atal Incubation Centres
schemes.tagline: Funds institutions with grants-in-aid of up to ₹10 crore to set up world-class, sector-specific startup incubation centres.
schemes.whatIsThis: Academic and non-academic institutions can apply to AIM to set up Atal Incubation Centres (AICs) that provide infrastructure and access to sectoral expertise to innovative startups.
schemes.objectives: Atal Incubation Centres (AICs) aim to promote and establish world-class, sector-specific incubation centres. The AICs provide physical infrastructure, access to sectoral experts for mentoring, business planning support, access to seed capital, industry partners, and capacity building, among others to encourage innovative startups.
eligibility: Academic institutions such as higher educational institutes, R&D institutes, etc.
Non-academic institutions such as corporate sector enterprises, alternative investment funds registered with Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI),
Business accelerators, groups of individuals, and individuals, etc. are eligible to apply
benefits: Successful applicants would be provided grants-in-aid to set up the AIC for a period of 5 years to cover the capital and operational expenditures.
Up to ₹5 crore (in case no lab facilities are established) and Up to ₹10 crore (in case of sector-specific lab facilities are established)
In the case of government applicants, no matching contribution is applicable.
In the case of non-government applicants, matching grants-in-aid shall be applicable in the range of 25% to 100%, based on the nature of the applicant.
schemes.howToApply: Interested applicants may visit https://aim.gov.in/aic-overview.php to know more.
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹10 crore per incubator, subject to guidelines

schemes.name: Atal Community Innovation Centres
schemes.tagline: Grants institutions up to ₹2.5 crore to set up community innovation centres for entrepreneurs in underserved regions.
schemes.whatIsThis: Academic and non-academic institutions can apply to AIM to set up ACICs that provide infrastructure facilities and capacity building opportunities to entrepreneurs in aspiring cities.
schemes.objectives: Atal Community Innovation Centres (ACIC) are supported by the Atal Innovation Mission (AIM). It aims to offer infrastructure facilities and capacity building opportunities to potential entrepreneurs in aspiring regions.
eligibility: The following academic and non-academic applicants with a minimum of 3 years of existence are eligible to apply:
Academic applicants: UGC and AICTE affiliated Universities / College; Educational Institutions like ITIs & other technical diploma colleges.
Non-academic applicants: Voluntary and other organizations with strong experience in the promotion of Science and Technology.
benefits: Support up to ₹2.5 crore in multiple tranches, based on the approved budget made by the applicant.
Funding from AIM to cover 50% of total project.
Applicant to match the remaining contribution either through PPP or private contribution.
schemes.howToApply:
Interested applicants may submit their applications online via https://aim.gov.in/
The applications are evaluated a Screening and Selection Committee.
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹2.5 crore over prescribed period

schemes.name: Scheme for Scale-up Support to Established Incubation Centres
schemes.tagline: Gives established incubators grant-in-aid of up to ₹10 crore to expand space, teams, equipment and seed funding for more startups.
schemes.whatIsThis: This Scheme helps established incubators expand their space, services, funding support, and infrastructure so they can support more startups.
schemes.objectives:
Established Incubation Centres (EICs) are existing incubators who are supported by the AIM to further enhance their incubation capacity.
The aim is to refurbish the existing incubation space, enhance capabilities and capacity, including team, infrastructure, tools and equipment, services, seed funding support, outreach initiatives, training programs and/or other incubation related events.
eligibility: EICs registered in India as a legal entity in public, private or public-private partnership mode.
Operational for a minimum of three years as of 1st August 2017.
benefits: Grant-in-aid support of up to ₹10 crore will be provided to EICs.
Balanced utilisation of fund as per suggestive activity-wise breakup is to be ensured.
EICs would be eligible to apply and avail grant-in-aid under this scheme for a maximum of three times.
schemes.howToApply: Interested applicants may visit https://aim.gov.in/atal-incubation-centres.php to know more
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹10 crore grant; matching private contribution required

schemes.name: Agriculture Infrastructure Financing Facility
schemes.tagline: 3% per annum interest subvention on loans up to ₹2 crore for post-harvest infrastructure and community farming projects, for up to 7 years.
schemes.whatIsThis: The facility offers an interest subvention of 3% per annum on loans for viable projects focused on post-harvest management infrastructure and community farming assets.
schemes.objectives: Aims to mobilise medium-long term debt financing facility for investment in viable projects for post-harvest management infrastructure and community farming assets through incentives and financial support.
eligibility: Funds to be provided by banks and financial institutions as loans to Primary Agricultural Credit Societies (PACS), Marketing Cooperative Societies, Farmer Producers Organizations(FPOs), Self Help Group (SHG), Farmers, Joint Liability Groups (JLG), Multipurpose Cooperative Societies, Agri-entrepreneurs, Startups and Central/State agency or Local Body sponsored Public Private Partnership Projects, State Agencies, Agricultural Produce Market Committees (Mandis), National & State Federations of Cooperatives, Federations of FPOs (Farmer Produce Organizations) and Federations of Self Help Groups (SHGs)
benefits: Interest subvention of 3% per annum on loans up to ₹2 crore, available for a maximum period of 7 years.
schemes.howToApply:
Applications are open round the year.
Visit https://agriinfra.dac.gov.in/Home/BeneficiaryRegistration
schemes.maxFunding: 3% interest subvention on loans up to ₹2 crore
schemes.howMuch: Loans up to ₹2 crore eligible for 3% interest subvention; scheme corpus ₹1 lakh crore

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana
schemes.tagline: Collateral-free Mudra loans up to ₹20 lakh from banks, NBFCs and MFIs to start, run or expand a small business.
schemes.whatIsThis: PMMY helps small businesses and entrepreneurs get collateral-free loans so they can start, run, or expand income-generating activities.
schemes.objectives: MUDRA is a refinancing Institution. It provides loans of up to ₹20 lakh to income generating micro/small business entities engaged in trading, manufacturing, service, activities allied to agriculture, poultry, farming etc.
eligibility: Individuals, Proprietary, Partnership Firm, Private Ltd. Company, Public Company, any other legal forms.
benefits: Term Loan / Overdraft/ Working Capital / Composite Loan for acquiring assets are required.
Loans shall be granted across the following types of loans:
Shishu Loan: up to ₹50,000
Kishor Loan: ₹50,000 to ₹5 lakh
Tarun Loan: ₹5 lakh to ₹10 lakh
TarunPLus Loan: ₹10 lakh to ₹20 lakh.
schemes.howToApply:
Mudra loans are available at all bank branches across the country. Mudra loans are also issued by NBFCs / MFIs who are engaged in financing for micro enterprises in small business activities.
It can also be applied through the Jan Samarth Business Loan Portal (https://www.jansamarth.in/business-loan-pradhan-mantri-mudra-yojana-scheme) or Udyamimitra portal (www.udyamimitra.in).
schemes.howMuch: Loans up to ₹10 lakh; Tarun Plus up to ₹20 lakh for eligible repeat borrowers

schemes.name: Stand-Up India
schemes.tagline: Bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore for SC/ST and women entrepreneurs setting up their first greenfield business.
schemes.whatIsThis: Stand-Up India helps SC/ST and women entrepreneurs get bank loans to start a new business for the first time.
schemes.objectives:
The Scheme aims to finance Scheduled Caste (SC)/ Scheduled Tribe (ST) and/or women entrepreneurs by facilitating bank loans for setting up a greenfield project enterprise in manufacturing, services, trading sector and activities allied to agriculture.
The objective is to facilitate bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore to at least one SC or ST entrepreneur and at least one-woman entrepreneur per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise.
This enterprise may be in manufacturing, services, agri-allied activities or the trading sector.
eligibility: Applicant must be from the SC/ST category or a woman, above 18 years of age. Finance is provided only for greenfield enterprises.
benefits: Eligible applicants are provided composite loan (inclusive of term loan and working capital) above ₹10 lakh and up to ₹1 crore.
The loan is repayable in 7 years with a maximum moratorium period of 18 months.
schemes.howToApply: The Stand-Up India Scheme was launched on 05.04.2016 and was valid/operational till March 2025.
schemes.maxFunding: Loans above ₹10 lakh and up to ₹1 crore
schemes.howMuch: ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore loan

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises Scheme
schemes.tagline: Gives micro food processing units a 35% capital subsidy up to ₹10 lakh, plus training, seed capital for SHGs and incubation support.
schemes.whatIsThis: PMFME helps small food processing businesses become formal, improve their products, and grow by giving them financial support, training, and access to incubation facilities.
schemes.objectives:
The Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME) Scheme promotes the formalisation of the unorganized microenterprises in the food processing sector through the establishment of Incubation Center based on One District One Product (ODOP).
To support establishment and utilization of Incubation Centers for ODOP and other products to be used on commercial basis
To provide training and capacity building support micro entrepreneurs/ SHGs/FPOs/Cooperatives and groups through Incubation Centers at the training rates prescribed under PMFME Scheme based on National Skill Framework.
eligibility: Existing individual microentrepreneurs/micro units/ SHGs/ FPOs/ Individuals.
benefits: Individual Units are provided with credit linked capital subsidy at 35% of the project cost with the maximum ceiling of ₹10 lakh per unit.
FPOs and Producer Cooperatives are provided with 35% grant with credit linkage.
SHGs are provided with seed capital of ₹40,000 per member for working capital and purchase of small tools.
Support through incubation centres.
schemes.howToApply: To know more: https://pmfme.mofpi.gov.in/pmfme/#/Login
schemes.maxFunding: 35% credit-linked subsidy up to ₹10 lakh per unit
schemes.howMuch: 35% credit-linked subsidy up to ₹10 lakh per unit; SHG seed capital up to ₹40,000/member

schemes.name: IFSCA Fintech Incentive Scheme
schemes.tagline: Grants of up to ₹75 lakh for fintech startups at GIFT IFSC to build products, run PoCs and sandboxes, and go to market.
schemes.whatIsThis: The IFSCA Fintech Incentive Scheme gives grants to fintech startups to help them build products, test solutions, and grow from prototype to market at GIFT IFSC.
schemes.objectives:
The IFSCA Fintech Incentive Scheme aims to promote the establishment of a world class FinTech Hub, at GIFT International Financial Services Centre (IFSC) India by providing financial support to the eligible fintech entities.
The Fintech Startup Grant is to be utilized for developing a product or a service and related ‘go-to market’ initiatives for a startup with a novel FinTech idea or solution.
eligibility: FinTech startup with a novel FinTech idea or solution with the focus on converting that idea into a Minimum Viable Product.
benefits: Fintech Startup Grant of up to ₹15 lakh
Proof of Concept (PoC) grant of up to ₹50 lakh
Sand-box grant of up to ₹30 lakh
Green FinTech Grant of up to ₹75 lakh
For accelerators - Accelerator Grant of up to ₹10 lakh.
schemes.howToApply: To know more: https://ifsca.gov.in/Pages/Contents/HowToApply
schemes.howMuch: Up to ₹75 lakh in many grant categories; varies by incentive type

schemes.name: Technology Development-cum-Incubation Centers
schemes.tagline: DAE incubation centres give startups and MSMEs prototyping facilities, test beds, and mentors to commercialise nuclear-sector tech.
schemes.whatIsThis: Technology Development-cum-Incubation Centers provide startups and industries research facilities, and mentoring to turn strong technology ideas into real products and businesses.
schemes.objectives:
The aim is to link India’s robust startup ecosystem to the nuclear sector by setting up Technology Development-cum-Incubation Centers for fostering synergy between research facilities and tech-entrepreneurs.
The main objective of Incubation Centres is to foster nucleation, incubation and growth of startups/enterprises based on technologies or available know-how.
eligibility: Startups/MSMEs/Industries that have robust proposals, based on sound original ideas.
benefits: Providing infrastructure and value-added services through prototyping facilities, test beds, pilot implementation, etc. to Indian industries; and creating a network of mentors who would provide sector-specific knowledge and real-world practical guidance.
Interested applicants are encouraged to visit the nearest incubation centres to collect more information on the Call for Proposals and benefits being provided.
schemes.howToApply:
Interested stakeholder can visit the different Technology Development-cum-Incubation Centers for more details on benefits available and application process.
For example, in the case of the Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research (IGCAR), eligible applicants can submit their proposal along with an Expression of Interest for incubation of the technology to Head, IC-IGCAR (incubation@igcar.gov.in).
schemes.howMuch: No standard grant; support is incubation / technology access based

schemes.name: Start-up Village Entrepreneurship Programme
schemes.tagline: Gives rural SHG members local business support and Community Enterprise Fund capital to start small non-farm businesses.
schemes.whatIsThis: SVEP helps rural SHG members and their families start small non-farm businesses by giving them local business support and access to capital.
schemes.objectives:
Startup Village Entrepreneurship Programme (SVEP), the sub-scheme under Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) supports members of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) or their family members to set-up small enterprises in the non-farm sector.
The ecosystem comprises of a cadre of Community Resource Person - Enterprise Promotion (CRP-EP) who will provide business support services to entrepreneurs.
eligibility: States/Union Territories (UTs) select blocks for the implementation on the SVEP which meet detailed criteria as defined in the guidelines.
Early-stage rural enterprises are the beneficiaries under the programme.
benefits: A dedicated fund called Community Enterprise Fund (CEF) is set-up to provide capital for enterprises.
schemes.howToApply: Apply here: https://svep.nrlm.gov.in/landing
schemes.howMuch: Support varies by block and enterprise plan; up to ₹6.5 crore per block overall

schemes.name: Venture Capital Fund for Scheduled Castes
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.tagline: Concessional loans of ₹10 lakh to ₹15 crore at a 4% coupon rate for businesses majority-owned by Scheduled Caste entrepreneurs.
schemes.whatIsThis: The Venture Capital Fund for Scheduled Castes (VCF-SC) helps SC entrepreneurs get affordable funding to start or grow businesses.
schemes.objectives:
Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment is implementing a Venture Capital Fund for Scheduled Castes with a corpus of ₹750 crore, intended for financial assistance to Scheduled Caste (VCF-SC) Entrepreneurs.
It is aimed at providing concessional finance to SC entrepreneurs, who will create wealth and value for society and at the same time will promote profitable businesses. This fund is managed by IFCI Venture Capital Ltd.
eligibility: The projects/units being set up in manufacturing, services and allied sector, including startups and units being incubated in the technology business incubators, ensuring asset creation out of the funds deployed in the unit shall be considered.
For Companies applying for assistance up to ₹50 lakh: Companies having at least 51% shareholding by Scheduled Castes entrepreneurs for the past 6 months with management control.

For Companies applying for assistance above ₹50 lakh: Companies having at least 51% shareholding by Scheduled Castes (SC) entrepreneurs for the past 12 months for the past 12 months with management control.
benefits: Loans from ₹10 lakh to ₹15 crore to SC entrepreneurs, at a 4% coupon rate.
For financial assistance up to ₹5 crore, up to 75% of the project cost are funded.
For financial assistance above ₹5 crore, up to 50% of the project cost are funded.
schemes.howToApply: Apply here: https://vcfsc.in/apply-online.html
schemes.howMuch: ₹10 lakh to ₹15 crore, subject to fund guidelines

schemes.name: Science Technology and Innovation (STI) Hubs for Development of Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) Communities
schemes.tagline: Funds academic and S&T institutions to set up STI Hubs that improve SC and ST community livelihoods through technology.
schemes.whatIsThis: STI Hubs support local institutions in using science, technology, and innovation to improve livelihoods, preserve community knowledge, and create practical development solutions for SC and ST communities.
schemes.objectives:
To capture the weakest linkages in the predominant livelihood systems of the SC and ST Communities and improving the livelihoods through Science and Technological intervention.
To harness, document, research, validate and conserve the Indigenous Knowledge Systems (IKS), skills and practices of different communities through inputs of science and technology.
eligibility: Local Academic or S&T institutions, R&D Labs, S&T based Voluntary Organizations (NGOs) etc. having majority of SC or ST population in their catchment area.
benefits: Financial support to establish an STI Hub.
This support can be used for equipment purchase, creation of small infrastructure, and common facility centres / construction costs for setting up the hub, as per DST norms.
schemes.howToApply: Please visit the DST website to know more: https://dst.gov.in/callforproposals/science-technology-and-innovation-sti-hubs-development-scheduled-caste-sc-and
schemes.howMuch: Project-based; amount varies by sanctioned STI hub

schemes.name: National Livestock Mission
schemes.tagline: 100% central assistance for extension, R&D and innovation in livestock, plus startup incentives and a Grand Challenge programme.
schemes.whatIsThis: The National Livestock Mission helps startups and institutions in animal husbandry build businesses, improve innovation, and strengthen the livestock value chain from production to market.
schemes.objectives: The National Livestock Mission (NLM) Scheme aims to develop entrepreneurs in the sector of animal husbandry in order to create the forward and backward linkages for the produce available at the unorganized sector with the organized sector.
eligibility: Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) Institutes, Central Institutes, State Government University farms and other credible institutions and startups
benefits: The Central Government will provide 100% assistance for conducting Extension, R&D and Innovation activities, based on the need and scope of the proposal shared by the applicant. The startups will also be incentivized for problem solving in the sheep, goat, poultry, pig, feed and fodder. The Department will conduct a Startup Grand Challenge Programme.
schemes.howToApply: The Central Agencies/ University farms will submit the proposal to the NLM division of the Department through their parent organization/ State Animal Husbandry Department. The parent organization/ Department shall approve and recommend the project before forwarding. Applications can be applied on the portal: https://nlm.udyamimitra.in/Login/Login
schemes.howMuch: 50% capital subsidy up to ₹50 lakh depending on project category

schemes.name: Mission on Advanced and High-Impact Research
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Power and Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
schemes.tagline: Project-based financial support to research, develop and demonstrate emerging power and clean-energy technologies at scale.
schemes.whatIsThis: MAHIR supports startups, industry, and research institutions in developing and testing advanced power and clean-energy technologies.
schemes.objectives: The Ministry of Power and Ministry of New and Renewable Energy have launched a National Mission, titled “Mission on Advanced and High-Impact Research (MAHIR)”, to identify emerging technologies in the power sector, to facilitate indigenous research, development, and demonstration of said latest and emerging technologies, at scale, for deployment within and outside India.
eligibility: Academia, Industry, Startups and R&D Institutions, and similar entities, working in the fields: Carbon Capture & Utilization, Green hydrogen for mobility (High Efficiency Fuel Cell), Automation and Artificial Intelligence: indigenous development of the eco-system, Geothermal Energy, Alternatives to Lithium-Ion storage batteries.
benefits: The financial support rendered under this program is subjective to the nature of the project, budgetary requirements of the same, the infrastructure required to implement it, in addition to the research area it falls under.
schemes.howToApply: Interested applicants may learn more about the initiative and its application process on https://cpri.res.in/sites/default/files/MAHIR_Call%20for%20proposals.pdf
schemes.howMuch: Project-based; mission funded through MoP/MNRE/CPSE pooled resources

schemes.name: Yuva Sahakar – Cooperative Enterprise Support and Innovation Scheme
schemes.tagline: Long-term finance with a 2% interest concession on term loans for newly formed cooperative societies with innovative ideas.
schemes.whatIsThis: Yuva Sahakar helps newly formed cooperative societies get easier long-term finance and interest support.
schemes.objectives:
The Scheme focuses on supporting newly formed cooperative societies with innovative and value chain–enhancing ideas through financial assistance and incentives.
The scheme is linked with a dedicated Cooperative Startup and Innovation Fund and provides special encouragement to cooperatives in Northeastern Region, Aspirational Districts, and those led by women, SC/ST, and PwD members.
The aim is to encourage young entrepreneurs to establish and manage cooperative enterprises. And to strengthen the cooperative ecosystem by enabling sustainable and scalable projects.
eligibility: Newly formed cooperative societies.
benefits: Provides long-term financial support to cooperative startups.
Offers a 2% interest concession on term loans.
Encourages innovation and new business ideas.
Supports young entrepreneurs in the cooperative sector.
Allows convergence with other government subsidies.
schemes.howToApply: https://www.ncdc.in/documents/other/3908071119Yuva.pdf
schemes.howMuch: Loan up to project norms; often up to ₹3 crore project cost reference; 2% interest subvention

schemes.name: Entrepreneurship and Skill Development Programme
schemes.ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.tagline: Business and technical skills training through three modules to help aspiring and existing entrepreneurs start and grow MSMEs.
schemes.whatIsThis: ESDP helps aspiring and existing entrepreneurs build business and practical skills so they can start, manage, and grow MSMEs.
schemes.objectives:
To motivate people from different sections of society to consider entrepreneurship as a career option, by imparting technical and business skills training.
To provide entrepreneurship training to entrepreneurs in domains including e-commerce, software, biotech, modern agriculture, genomics and others
To support the establishment of new MSMEs and enhance growth of existing ones.
eligibility: Existing or aspiring entrepreneurs.
40% of the beneficiaries are required to be from weaker sections of society including SC/ST/Women/Physically Handicapped.
benefits: Training is provided under three modules – Entrepreneurship Awareness Program, Entrepreneurship-cum-Skill Development Program, and Management Development Program.
Hands-on practice or demonstration to upgrade skills of prospective entrepreneurs.
schemes.howToApply: Apply through the websites of MSME-DFOs, MSME-TC and other Implementing Agencies under ESDP scheme
schemes.howMuch: No direct startup funding; training cost supported per programme norms
